name: Nattokinase
latinName: Bacillus subtilis var. natto (fermented soybean enzyme)
historicalUse: Natto — fermented soybeans — has been consumed in Japan since approximately the 10th century CE, traditionally associated with cardiovascular longevity in populations with high consumption. The enzyme nattokinase was isolated and characterized in 1980 by Dr. Hiroyuki Sumi who discovered its fibrinolytic activity. The traditional use of fermented foods for circulatory health appears across multiple classical traditions including Chinese medicine (fermented soybean preparations) and Middle Eastern fermented legume foods.
classicalSources: Traditional Japanese dietary medicine — natto consumption
modernEvidence: Nattokinase is a serine protease with direct fibrinolytic activity — it degrades fibrin clots and activates endogenous plasminogen activators. Clinical studies show significant reductions in whole blood viscosity, fibrinogen levels, and systolic blood pressure. NSK-SD® is a vitamin K2-free standardized form, removing concern about warfarin interaction from K2 content while preserving full enzymatic activity.
mechanisms: Direct fibrinolysis — cleaves fibrin clots more effectively than plasmin
Plasminogen activator stimulation — upregulates endogenous tPA and uPA
Antihypertensive — fibrinolytic activity reduces peripheral vascular resistance
PAI-1 inhibition — reduces plasminogen activator inhibitor, promoting fibrinolytic balance
benefits: Supports healthy blood viscosity and flow
Supports healthy blood pressure already within normal range
safetyProfile: Well tolerated. NSK-SD® is vitamin K2-free, eliminating a key safety concern. Avoid in patients at high bleeding risk. Should be discontinued prior to surgery.
contraindications: Recent stroke — consult physician before use
drugInteractions: CRITICAL — Anticoagulants (warfarin, heparin, novel oral anticoagulants): additive fibrinolytic effect; potential for significant bleeding; contraindicated without physician oversight
Antiplatelet agents (aspirin, clopidogrel) — additive effect; monitor closely
physicianNotes: Nattokinase carries the most significant drug interaction risk of ingredients in the circulatory formulation. Contraindicated in patients on anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy without physician oversight and close monitoring. Discontinue 2 weeks pre-operatively. NSK-SD® form is preferred as it eliminates vitamin K2 content. Appropriate for healthy adults seeking circulatory terrain support without concurrent anticoagulant therapy.
patientGuidance: IMPORTANT: If you take any blood-thinning medications including warfarin, aspirin, clopidogrel, or newer anticoagulants (Xarelto, Eliquis, Pradaxa), do NOT use this product without explicit guidance from your physician. Discontinue use at least 2 weeks before any planned surgery.
